memoryArtifacts.description:
MAY 5, 2017

THOMAS CRANMER, ARCHBISHOP OF CANTERBURY, was born 2 July 1489, at Aslacton, Watton Parish, Nottinghamshire, England, to Thomas Cranmer Esq (1468-1501) and Agnes Hatfield (1470-1556.)  He …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Cranmer於1536年出生在英國英格蘭肯特坎特布瑞時，她的父親Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury45歲，母親Alice Ann Sands34歲。她於大約1556年與Thomas Norton在英國英格蘭貝德福爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她於1584年住在英國賀特福爾順特。她去世於1600年在英國英格蘭貝德福爾盧頓享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭貝德福爾斯特亞特利聖 馬加也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Cranmer于1536年出生在英国英格兰肯特坎特布瑞时，她的父亲Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury45岁，母亲Alice Ann Sands34岁。她于大约1556年与Thomas Norton在英国英格兰貝德福爾结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她于1584年住在英国賀特福爾順特。她去世于1600年在英国英格兰貝德福爾盧頓享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰貝德福爾斯特亞特利聖 馬加也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Cranmer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Cranmer Green in Walsham le Willows (Suffolk), and perhaps also from other places similarly named, such as Cranmer Hall in Sculthorpe (Norfolk), Cranmore in Shalfleet (Isle of Wight), Cranmer Bottom in Selborne and Cranmore in Aldershot (both Hampshire), and East and West Cranmore (Somerset), all named from Old English cran ‘crane’ + mere ‘lake, pool’ or mōr ‘moor, marshy ground’. See also Cranmore , with which there may have been some confusion.

variants.name: Cranmore
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Worcestershire): variant of   Cranmer  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Cranmer was born in 1536, in Canterbury, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury, was 45 and her mother, Alice Ann Sands, was 34. She married Thomas Norton about 1556, in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. She lived in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, England in 1584. She died in 1600, in Luton, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in St Margaret's Church, Streatley, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Cranmer est née en 1536, à Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Alice Ann Sands, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Norton environ 1556, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Elle a vécu à Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, Angleterre en 1584. Elle est décédée en 1600, à Luton, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à St Margaret's Church, Streatley, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Cranmer 1536 in Canterbury, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury 45 und ihre Mutter Alice Ann Sands 34. Sie hat um 1556 in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Norton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1584 hat sie in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, England gelebt. Sie starb 1600 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Luton, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St Margaret's Church, Streatley, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Cranmer è nata il nel 1536 nel Canterbury, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury, aveva 45 anni e sua madre, Alice Ann Sands, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Norton circa 1556, nel Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Vissuto a Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, England nel 1584. Morì nel 1600, nel Luton, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a St Margaret's Church, Streatley, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Cranmerが1536年にイギリスイギリスケントカンテルブリーで生まれた時，父Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterburyは45歳，母Alice Ann Sandsは34歳であった。約1556年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアでThomas Nortonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1584年にはイギリスハートフォードシュアイアチェシュントに住んでいた。彼女は1600年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアルトンで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアストレアトリースト マルガーエト'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Cranmer(이)가 1536년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 캉르버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Ann Sands(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1556년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 Thomas Norton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1584년도에 영국 허트퍼드실르 체슈운트에서 살았다. 그녀는 1600년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 루턴에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 스트리틀리 스트 마가트'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Cranmer nasceu em 1536, em Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Alice Ann Sands, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Norton aproximadamente 1556, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela viveu em Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra em 1584. Ela faleceu em 1600, em Luton, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em St Margaret's Church, Streatley, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Cranmer родилась в 1536 г. в местности кантирбури, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Ann Sands, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Norton около 1556 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она жила в местности чешант, хиртфордшир, Англия в 1584 году. Она умерла в 1600 г. в местности лутон, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности ст маргарет'с чурч, стритли, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Cranmer nació en 1536, en Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Edmund Cranmer Archdeacon of Canterbury, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Alice Ann Sands, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Thomas Norton aproximadamente 1556, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Vivió en Cheshunt, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra en 1584. Murió en 1600, en Luton, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en St Margaret's Church, Streatley, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
